output:
I’m locating the implementation for story `26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation:quality`, identifying the changed files, and then I’ll review those diffs against correctness, security, architecture, and AC coverage.I found the active implementation in the worktree rather than in committed history. Next I’m reading the story spec and the exact diffs in `workflow-runner` and its tests so I can review the behavior against the intended contract.I have the main diff. I’m reading the surrounding runner logic now to check whether these changes fit the state machine boundaries cleanly and whether the new behavior introduces regressions outside story 26-2.I’m checking the completion path now. One of the new changes writes a terminal snapshot in `complete`, and I need to confirm whether later cleanup still guarantees the expected “no snapshot after successful completion” behavior.The runner still clears persistence on clean success after the actor and sprint loop, so the extra terminal save doesn’t by itself violate the cleanup contract. I’m now checking the remaining two risky areas: whether the new hash scope is correct, and whether “restore missing disk completions after resume” can incorrectly skip work.I found one likely architectural fault line: `loadCheckpointLog()` is no longer used to rebuild completion state anywhere in `runWorkflowActor`. I’m checking whether that silently broke the later checkpoint-log resume path, because if it did, this story fix is bleeding across story boundaries.I confirmed one concrete regression: the checkpoint-log fallback path from story 26-3 has been removed and replaced with unconditional clearing when no snapshot exists. I’m checking whether there’s a second independent issue or whether the rest is mainly test/AC coverage risk.I have one implementation regression and one clear verification gap. I’m pulling exact line references from the proof doc so the review can distinguish between code defects and acceptance-coverage defects cleanly.I have enough for the review, but I’m grabbing the exact 26-3 spec lines that this change now contradicts so the architecture finding is anchored to project docs rather than inference.I found a stronger version of the coverage problem: the “matching hash” tests never provide a real snapshot object, so they don’t execute the actual resume branch into `createActor(..., { snapshot })`. I’m pulling those exact lines for the final review.<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. High: the current runner removes the checkpoint-log fallback instead of using it, which breaks the dual-layer resume architecture and will replay completed work after a config mismatch or a missing snapshot. In [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L135), the mismatch branch clears the checkpoint log, and in [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L151) the no-snapshot branch treats any checkpoint log as orphaned and deletes it. That directly contradicts the documented behavior in [26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md#L27) and [26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md#L149), which require loading the checkpoint log to rebuild `completedTasks` when snapshot resume is unavailable.

2. Medium: AC coverage for story 26-2 is not trustworthy because the tests do not exercise the actual snapshot-resume path. The runner only passes a snapshot into XState when `resumeSnapshot !== null` in [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L176), but the matching-hash tests feed `snapshot: null` in [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1554) and [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1615), so they only verify logging, not real resume behavior. One asserted AC is effectively a no-op (`expect(mismatchDiscards).toBeDefined()`) in [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1632). The proof then claims AC6-AC9 from inference rather than direct resumed-run tests in [26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md#L121). That leaves the key behaviors “completed tasks are not re-executed on resume”, “savedAt advances on resumed progress”, “cleanup/preservation after resumed completion/error”, and “multi-interrupt chain resumes from the latest snapshot” insufficiently verified.
</issues>

I did not find a distinct security bug in these changes; the failures are correctness and architecture/verification related.
